parsed_review.summary: CP00-237 (RP07 search-core model binding slice, risk B, 40 units RP07.P01.M04.S07-RP07.P01.M06.S04) is a sound descriptor-only closeout pack. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms: (1) Scope/coverage integrity — the closeout-pack-plan entry, review baseline, registry.js binding (SEARCH_CORE_CP237_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS), and contract (current_pack + model_binding_slice_descriptor + historical_pack_bindings tail + cp237_requirements/cp237_no_write_attestation) all agree on risk B, 40 units, the range, deliverable distribution (impl 22, ui 6, fixture 2, test 6, hermes 2, claude 2), and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 14, Permission And Audit Binding 22, Synthetic Fixture Set 4); the M04/M05/M06 row sets match the baseline included_units. (2) No-write/no-real-data — product source (registry/service/validators/index.js) is pure Object.freeze descriptor construction with zero I/O, network, db, child_process, or env access (grep-confirmed); only test/validator infra reads JSON. Attestations and validators enforce no search/OCR/index/embedding runtime, no permission/audit evaluation, no state/storage writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no raw payload, tenant cross-access=false, matter-trace required, ownership drift=false. (3) production_ready is correctly NOT claimed at this stage (command-evidence production_ready=false, gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending; Hermes uses production_ready_candidate); the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification milestone, not an enterprise-trust claim. (4) Authority boundary holds — Claude is read-only reviewer, not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false everywhere, human_final_approval_required=true, manifest claude_not_final_approver=true); Hermes H07 is a deterministic gate/receipt label only, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. (5) Review hardening — readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_tools_present=false; post-review artifacts (claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection, raw-output, receipt) are expectedly absent at this point in the documented hardened_review_sequence. All listed pre-review validation commands report exit 0; closeout-pack:validate and the review chain are correctly gated to post-review. Unrelated untracked workstream dirs were not attributed to this pack. One P3 cosmetic finding only (a carryover label on the contract's non-validated top-level no_write_attestation object); no P0/P1/P2. Does not block pack or goal closeout. Final enterprise-trust closure remains a human decision outside this receipt.

findings.title: Contract top-level no_write_attestation carries stale CP235 label on a CP00-237 object
findings.details: The contract's top-level `no_write_attestation` block (lines 32-52) has `pack_id: "CP00-237"` but is labeled `validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only: true` (the CP00-235 base-attestation label) and omits the `validates_model_storage_slice_descriptor_only`, `validates_model_binding_slice_descriptor_only`, and `ownership_drift_detected` fields that the pack-specific attestation carries. This is cosmetic only: every boundary fact the object states is true and accurate for CP00-237, the object is NOT deep-compared by any gate (the validator and model.test.js deep-equal only the authoritative `cp237_no_write_attestation` at lines 638-660, which is complete and correct), and the pattern matches prior packs. No functional, security, or boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally relabel the top-level rollup `no_write_attestation` to the model-binding-slice descriptor flag (or align its field set with `cp237_no_write_attestation`) for clarity in a future pack. No action required for CP00-237 closeout.
